items.title: The Morning Mist
items.description: Features Joe Burke on accordion and Charlie Lennon on piano. The title track was composed by Joe in 1958. The album features 21 tracks both old and new, recorded at Charlie's Cuan studio at Spiddal in Connemara.

items.title: Collection
items.description: A 20 track compilation of the first three Stockton's Wing albums: Stockton's Wing, Take A Chance, and Light In The Western Sky. Charts the early years from the band's inception in 1977 to their first major success.

items.title: The Sligo Style
items.description: A compilation focused on Sligo musicians and the traditional Irish fiddle music style associated with the Sligo region. Released by Sound Records, a label specializing in Irish Traditional Music.

items.title: In Full Spate
items.description: One of Paddy Glackin's seminal recordings featuring Donal Lunny on production and performance. Features traditional compositions arranged by both Glackin and Lunny.

items.title: The Lonesome Touch
items.description: The first recording together capturing the musical kinship between fiddle master Martin Hayes and guitarist Dennis Cahill. Features 12 tracks of traditional Irish tunes played at a slow tempo with beautiful, creative accompaniment.

items.title: Draíocht
items.description: Debut recording featuring harp and flute music. Draíocht (dree-ucht) means magic and druidry in Irish. Finished in the Irish Echo's top 20 trad albums for 2004. Michael is a harpist from Monaghan and June is a flutist from Sligo.

items.title: Yeh, that's all it is
items.description: Traditional Celtic music album by John Carty, one of Ireland's best traditional musicians. Features Carty mainly on fiddle backed by guitars, keyboards and banjo, delivering traditional Irish music rooted in the regional styles of Western Ireland.

items.title: The Collection
items.description: A compilation of studio recordings by legendary Irish folk singer and founding member of The Dubliners. Features Luke Kelly classics including 'Raglan Road', 'Song For Ireland', 'Scorn Not His Simplicity' and 'The Night Visiting Song'.

items.title: Chapter Eight
items.description: The eighth album from the world renowned Kilfenora Ceili Band featuring Don Stiffe. Features four fiddle players, two flautists, concertina, accordion, banjo, drums, piano, cello and double bass. Embraces both 'the needs of the dancer' and 'the needs of the concert patron'.

items.title: Lá an Dreoilín
items.description: Lá an Dreoilín (Wren Day) features 14 tracks recorded at Grouse Lodge Studios. The band includes Paul Finn on accordion and concertina, Séan McElwain on banjo and bouzouki, Tristan Rosenstock on bodhrán and vocals, Oisín Mac Diarmada on fiddle and vocals, and John Blake on flute and guitar.

items.title: It Was Mighty!
items.description: The Early Days of Irish music in London - Volume 29 in the Voice of the People series from Topic Records. A 3-CD set featuring over 100 vital performances compiled by Dr. Reg Hall. Documents Irish musicians from rural Ireland who found themselves in London in the 1950s.

items.title: More Irish Songs We Learned at School
items.description: Follow-up to the successful 'Irish Songs We Learned At School' featuring 13 tracks of traditional Irish folk songs. Features guest appearances by Christy Moore and Seán Ó Sé, and includes students of Coláiste Choilm and GaelCholáiste Choilm, Ballincollig, Co Cork.

items.title: Síimhín Sí
items.description: Traditional Irish music album by Aoife Granville featuring authentic Irish traditional music performance.

items.title: Otherworld
items.description: Lúnasa's second major release and first album released outside of Ireland. Became the fastest-selling album in the history of Green Linnet Records. Named 'Traditional Album of the Year' by Irish Echo and Irish Voice in 1999. Features 11 tracks with traditional Celtic sound incorporating unique techniques.

items.title: Finest
items.description: Collection of Sean McGuire recordings - The Definitive Collection. Features 18 tracks of traditional Irish tunes including reels, airs, hornpipes, and jigs showcasing Sean McGuire's virtuoso fiddle playing.

items.title: The Mountain Road
items.description: Compilation of tunes popular in South Sligo - the fourth recording under the Coleman Heritage label. Features almost 20 different musicians, mostly flute and fiddle players, playing their individual versions of tunes in natural settings including famous music pubs in the area.

items.title: The Story So Far...
items.description: The seventh album by Lúnasa - a best-of compilation with representative recordings from the band's first eleven years. Features 16 songs including re-recorded and remixed tracks. Includes expansive liner notes on the band's history and an extensive photo journal.

items.title: From Stage to Stage
items.description: Celebration of 21 years - a collectable CD/DVD companion set showcasing two great concerts from stages on opposite sides of the globe (Sebastopol CA and Sligo). Features guest appearances from Väsen, Mike Marshall, Martin Hayes, Dennis Cahill, Ron Sexsmith, and Duke Special.

items.title: Shannon's Lovely Vale
items.description: Traditional Irish ceili band music from the Shannonvale Ceili Band, continuing the proud tradition of Irish dance and traditional music.

items.title: The Leitrim Equation 3
items.description: The third CD in a series which evolved from Leitrim County Council's decision to invite well-known musicians to spend time in the county exploring its musical traditions. Features 18 songs celebrating the musical dialects of Leitrim. Includes many Leitrim guests.

items.title: The Man from Clare
items.description: Features Micho Russell (1915-1994), the legendary tin whistle player from Doonagore, Doolin, County Clare. Known for his minimal ornamentation and foot-tapping style. Won the All-Ireland tin whistle competition in 1973.

items.title: The Star Above the Garter
items.description: Celebrated Irish traditional fiddle album featuring brother and sister Denis Murphy and Julia Clifford from Sliabh Luachra. Just the two siblings' brilliant fiddles with no other accompaniment. Often ranked as the best Irish traditional fiddle duet album of all time.

items.title: The Gold Ring
items.description: 2-CD set with 65 tracks, over 2.5 hours of Willie Clancy's piping from a period of over twenty years. Willie Clancy (1918-73) was an iconic figure in Irish music from Miltown Malbay, Co. Clare. Selected by RTÉ radio presenter Peter Browne from RTÉ Libraries and Archives.

items.title: Concertina Music from West Clare
items.description: 31 tracks recorded by Ciaran Mac Mathuna in 1955 and 1960. Extensive remastering work by Harry Bradshaw in the RTÉ archives. Mrs. Crotty's lovely concertina playing features largely solo but occasionally in company with Paddy Canny, Denis Murphy, and Michael Tubridy.

items.title: Journeyman
items.description: Features long forgotten tunes from Gerry's home area in the North East of Ireland. Counted in the top five Albums of the year 2004 by the Irish Times. Gerry 'Fiddle' O'Connor from Dundalk, County Louth, founding member of Skylark and La Lúgh.

items.title: Farewell to Ireland
items.description: Four-CD set of Irish music recorded in New York in the 1920s and 1930s, compiled by Ron Kavana. The body of music is regarded by many as the lost treasure of the golden age of traditional Irish music. Features Michael Coleman, James Morrison, Paddy Killoran, and many others.

items.title: From Ballymote to Brooklyn
items.description: Collection of vintage recordings by these two great Sligo fiddlers, among the most influential artists in Irish music at the dawn of recording. Tracks remastered by Gregory Daly and P.J. Hernon. Paddy Killoran plays on tracks 1-14, James Morrison on tracks 15-24.

items.title: 60th Anniversary Celebration
items.description: Recorded in June 2006 to mark the Band's 60th Anniversary. Serves as a tribute to band founders J. Joe Hayes and Francie Donnellan. The Tulla Céilí Band is one of the finest céilí bands in the country.

items.title: Sing us a song Paddy
items.description: Paddy Berry (born October 12, 1937) is an Irish traditional singer from Wexford, Ireland. Renowned as one of the most respected ballad singers and songwriters of his generation and a great collector of folklore and song. Received a special lifetime achievement award from President Mary McAleese in 2003.

items.title: Caipíní
items.description: 74-minute album of traditional songs and music from West Kerry. The title means 'caps' and refers to the time when every man wore such headgear. Originated from a TG4 television programme co-produced by Brendan Begley with Niamh Ní Bhaoill and Philip King in 2000.

items.title: The Floating Bow
items.description: Recordings made by Alun Evans between 1968 and 1974 at a farmhouse in Glenconwell, Ardara, County Donegal. These recordings were made when John was at his musical best and are considered some of the most important fiddle tracks in the Irish tradition.

items.title: Mícheál Ó Súilleabháin
items.description: Debut album released when Ó Súilleabháin became a music lecturer at UCC in 1975. Collection of solo interpretations of traditional music on piano, clavichord, harmonium and Moog synthesiser. Mícheál (1950-2018) founded the Irish World Academy of Music and Dance at University of Limerick.

items.title: Past Masters of Irish Dance Music
items.description: Anthology of re-mastered 78 rpm recordings illustrating the rich diversity of Irish dance music played in Ireland, Britain and America in the first half of the twentieth century. Features Patrick Killoran, Seamus Ennis, James Morrison, and others. Edited by Reg Hall.

items.title: Seoltaí Séidte
items.description: 2-CD set released to commemorate Gael Linn's Golden Jubilee. Compiles all twenty of Gael-Linn's historic 78 rpm discs issued between 1957 to 1961. 43 performances by outstanding solo singers in Irish from Irish-speaking districts and solo musicians. 96-page booklet compiled by Nicholas Carolan.

items.title: The Fiddler's Delight
items.description: Collection of twenty-four tracks of un-reissued recordings of great traditional Irish fiddle playing from 1921-1945, featuring artists like Hugh Gillespie, James Morrison, Michael Coleman, and Paddy Killoran. 36-page booklet included.

items.title: The De Cuellar Suite
items.description: Based on the story of Captain Francisco de Cuéllar, one of the few survivors from the Spanish Armada ships which foundered off Streedagh Strand, Sligo in 1588. Collaboration of Irish Traditional, Galician Traditional and Classical musicians. 11 movements, live recording of premiere featuring over 65 musicians.

items.title: The Belfast Harp Festival
items.description: Double-CD commemorating the 200th anniversary of the 1792 harpers festival. Contains 22 examples from the repertoire of traditional harpers plus 20 harp solos and songs from Carolan's extensive output. Gráinne Yeats (1925-2013) was one of the first to revive the wire-strung harp.

items.title: Carolan's Receipt
items.description: Recorded in 1975, the same year Bell joined the Chieftains. One of the first LPs devoted to the music of blind harper Turlough O'Carolan. First recorded performance on the brass wire harp. Features The Chieftains on several tracks.

items.title: Carolan's Favourite
items.description: Derek Bell's second Carolan album, paying tribute to Turlough O'Carolan (1670-1738). Features Derek joined by The Chieftains and The New Irish Chamber Orchestra.

items.title: Feasting with Carolan
items.description: Turlough O Carolan provided the inspiration and almost all the melodic material for Clarsheree's first album. Clarsheree consisted of Janet Harbison with 4 students. All music arranged and produced by Janet Harbison.

items.title: Across the Black River
items.description: First release from Loftus Music featuring collaboration between fiddler Kevin Burke and guitarist/composer Cal Scott. Selected by the New York Times as one of the top World Music releases of the year.

items.title: Feadóga Stáin
items.description: Mary Bergin's debut solo album, credited with establishing the tin whistle as one of the first-tier instruments of Irish Traditional Music. Critically cited as outstanding and unequalled, considered a definitive or milestone album. Cited by Joanie Madden as highly influential.

items.title: Now She's Purring
items.description: Features two virtuosos of Sliabh Luachra music who join together on flute and fiddle. Gerry Harrington and Billy Clifford have had a lifelong affinity with the music of Sliabh Luachra. Billy Clifford helped to define the local flute style. Released July 9, 2018 with fifteen tracks.

items.title: The Rest is History
items.description: Released in 2014 following their All-Ireland title, the Moylurg Céili Band's first CD received plaudits from all quarters of the traditional music world. Launched by RTÉ's Kieran Hanrahan at Kilronan Castle, Co. Roscommon. Features musicians from counties Roscommon, Leitrim, Galway, Sligo, and Donegal.

items.title: Santiago
items.description: Album dedicated to traditional music of Galicia, the region in northwest Spain, and adaptation of Galician emigrants' musical folklore in Latin American music. Features collaborations with Carlos Núñez, Linda Ronstadt, Los Lobos, Ry Cooder. Won the 1997 Grammy Award for Best World Music Album.

items.title: Flatfish
items.description: The second album and debut studio recording by Anglo-Irish celtic music group Flook, released in 1999. Features nine tracks with performers John Joe Kelly (bodhrán), Sarah Allen (flute, whistle, accordion), Brian Finnegan (flute, whistle), and Ed Boyd (guitar, bouzouki, mandolin). Received accolades including 'stunning', 'remarkable' and 'little short of miraculous'.

items.title: The Home Ruler
items.description: Released in September 2008, Catherine McEvoy's third album featuring 15 tracks of Sligo-Roscommon style flute playing. Catherine is considered one of the best exponents of this style. Accompanied by Paddy McEvoy, Felix Dolan, Geraldine Cotter, Steve Cooney and Joe Kennedy. Album named after the iconic hornpipe.

items.title: Making Tracks
items.description: The Dartry Céilí Band's second album released in 2014, received excellent reviews throughout the world. The band won the All Ireland Senior Céilí Band competition at Fleadh Cheoil na hÉireann in Tullamore in 2009. The 11-piece band features musicians from North Connacht with Philip Duffy as band leader.

items.title: Tús Nua
items.description: Debut album by Gatehouse released September 16, 2016 on Gael Linn. The name means 'Fresh Start'. The group consists of Rachel Garvey (vocals), Jacinta McEvoy (guitar, concertina), John McEvoy (fiddle, mandola), and John Wynne (flute, whistles). Songs and tunes grounded in the North Connaught tradition. Irish Times praised Rachel Garvey's remarkable voice.

items.title: The Past is Another Tune
items.description: Edited by Gregory Daly and released by the Coleman Heritage Centre in 2018. Part of the Coleman Archive series documenting traditional Irish music from the Coleman Irish Music Centre in Gurteen, County Sligo.

items.title: It's not Racket Science
items.description: Released September 1, 2008 with thirteen tracks featuring John Carty on fiddle and banjo, Brian McGrath on piano and tenor guitar, Seamus O'Donnell on sax, flute and vocals, and Michael McCague on guitar and bouzouki. Racket Records is an independent family run record label based in northwest Ireland.

items.title: The Flute Players of Roscommon Volume 2
items.description: Produced by John Wynne and released by Feadog Mor productions in 2008. Features Aidan and Breda Shannon, James Carty, Packie Duignan, Michael Anthony Glynn, Finbarr McGreevy, Tom McHale, Liz Wynne, and many other traditional Irish flute players from County Roscommon.

items.title: Drivin' Leitrim Timber
items.description: Released September 1, 2010 with 13 tracks. A 'master-class' in flute playing, all with a strong sympathetic accompaniment. Dave Sheridan is a multi-instrumentalist from Killargue. Hot Press Magazine described his flute playing as having 'a highly accomplished free flowing fluid style'.

items.title: The Raineys
items.description: Recordings from the Pavee Point Travellers Centre featuring Paddy and Stephen Rainey on fiddle recorded in Freeney's Pub in Letterfrack, Connemara, in 1956. Sister Brigid sings three songs. The Raineys were Travellers who based themselves in Tuam during winter but toured the fairs and markets of Connemara during summer.

items.title: Tuning the Radio
items.description: Traditional Folk album released by RTÉ in 2010 featuring 25 songs. The traditional music acetates in the RTÉ collection were transferred to a digitized format. Three recordings from 1940 are included: The Kincora Ceili Band, Seamus Ennis (uilleann pipes), and The Dublin Metropolitan Garda Ceili Band. Largely the work of Peter Browne.

items.title: Fiddle Music of Donegal Volume 2
items.description: Released in 1997 by Cairdeas Recordings, this volume features fiddle music from County Donegal performed by Roisin McGrory and Damian Harrigan, documenting the distinctive fiddle tradition of the region.

items.title: The Torn Jacket
items.description: Tommy Guihen's first solo album released in 2017 despite winning the senior all-Ireland flute title in 1978. From Bodorragha, between Keadue and Arigna in north Co. Roscommon. Features reels, jigs, two slow airs, hornpipes and a setting of 'Eleanor Plunkett'. #6 on London Top Ten 2018, #3 on Ennis Top Ten.

items.title: Round the House and Mind the Dresser
items.description: Compilation of old recordings edited by Reg Hall, released by Topic Records in 2001. Outstanding collection focusing on polkas, slides, flings, hornpipes, mazurkas, waltzes and barndances. One-third from remastered 78s (1925-1950s), balance from 60s-70s recordings. Features Michael Coleman, Johnny Doherty, Johnny O'Leary, and others.

items.title: Irish Traditional Music
items.description: Three CDs of tunes played slowly and at 'session' tempo recorded by Sarah-Jane Woods, Michael Harrison, Aoife Johnston, Oisín Chambers, Janine Redmond, Niamh Denmead, Emma Woods. Published by Craobh Naithí (Dublin) branch of Comhaltas in 2007. This is a teaching and learning resource for traditional Irish music.

items.title: The Blackberry Blossom
items.description: Released in 2000 featuring elegant, restrained, soulful concertina playing from East Clare. Mary MacNamara from Tulla is accompanied by Geraldine Cotter, Catherine McEvoy, and Andrew MacNamara. 15 tracks showcasing the East Clare concertina tradition.

items.title: The Far Side of the Glen
items.description: Released in 2011 featuring traditional Irish fiddle music from southwest Donegal. Derek is from Glencolmcille and Tara from Glenties. The title reflects their birthplaces on either side of the Glengesh Pass. Both learned from the late James Byrne of Glencolmcille. Recorded over two days in a cottage near Carrick, County Donegal.

items.title: Feadóga Stáin 2
items.description: Mary Bergin's second tin whistle album, a follow-up to her landmark 1979 debut. Features Joe McKenna on uilleann pipes. Produced by Mary Bergin with sleeve notes by Jackie Small. Continues her mastery of the instrument that established the tin whistle as a first-tier instrument in Irish traditional music.

items.title: Oldtime Records Vol 1
items.description: Compilation of reissued 78rpm recordings from the golden era of Irish-American music. Features historic recordings by Louis E. Quinn & His Shamrock Minstrels, Flanagan Brothers, James Morrison, Paddy Killoran, Hugh Gillespie, and other notable Irish-American musicians from the early 20th century.

items.title: Oldtime Records Vol 2
items.description: Second volume of reissued 78rpm recordings from Irish-American musicians. Features Sullivan's Shamrock Band, Paddy Killoran, Michael Coleman, John McKenna, James Morrison, and other legendary performers. Includes notable tracks like Coleman's 'Prohibition Reel' and McKenna's 'Roscommon Reels'.

items.title: Níl Gar Ann
items.description: Archival recordings of Frank Cassidy (1900-1971) from Teelin, Co. Donegal, one of the finest fiddlers of his generation. Features 35 tracks over 59 minutes including solo fiddle and 5 duets with John Doherty. The title comes from Cassidy's modest exclamation 'Níl gar ann!' ('It's no use!') when Séamus Ennis tried to record him.

items.title: Irish Dance Music
items.description: Edited by Reg Hall, this anthology features remastered 78rpm recordings made between 1922-1948 in New York, Chicago, Dublin, and London. Two dozen recordings illustrating traditional dance-music repertoires, styles and contexts. Features fiddlers, accordionists and flute-players including Michael Coleman and Frank Quinn.

items.title: Ó Riada sa Gaiety
items.description: One of the most important albums in Irish traditional music history. Recorded live at Dublin's Gaiety Theatre in 1969, paying homage to Ulster poet Peadar Ó Doirnín. Features the ensemble that became the root of The Chieftains, including Paddy Moloney, Martin Fay, and Sean Keane. A social and musical victory for traditional music.

items.title: Éistigh Seal
items.description: Re-issue of Cranitch's 1984 album of slow airs on fiddle, accompanied by his sister Bríd on piano. Recorded at Stiúideo an tSuláin, Baile Mhuirne. Drawing on influences from sean-nós singing, this album has been called 'quite possibly my favorite album of any music anywhere' and a welcome antidote to overly-flashy playing.

items.title: Farewell to Music
items.description: A collection of slow airs recorded before Tony MacMahon (1939-2021) retired from public performance due to a hand tremor. Coaxed out of him in 2009 by fiddler Caoimhín Ó Raghallaigh. Across thirteen previously unreleased tracks, the album represents the profound beauty and unsentimental empathy Tony achieved in his slow air playing.

items.title: A Feeling in the Blood
items.description: Comprehensive 3-CD set with over 3 hours of archival recordings of Néillidh Boyle (1889-1961) from Dungloe, Co. Donegal. Born in Pennsylvania to immigrant parents, he returned to Donegal at age eight and became regarded as a fiddler of unheard mastery. Includes a 34-page booklet with recordings from RTÉ, BBC, and the Alan Lomax Archive.

items.title: I gCnoc na Graí
items.description: A 1985 landmark recording where accordion and concertina worked in unison to spectacular effect. Recorded live over a weekend in October in Dan Connell's pub in Knocknagree, Co. Cork. Features 15 tracks including 'The Humours of Castlefin' and 'Port Na bPúcaí'. Notes by Peadar Ó Riada.

items.title: Prosperous
items.description: The first release on the iconic Tara Music label, named after the town and house in County Kildare where it was recorded. The line-up included Liam O'Flynn, Andy Irvine, and Donal Lunny, who later went on to become the revered Planxty. Originally recorded by Bill Leader and first issued in the UK on the Trailer label.

items.title: The Francis O'Neill Cylinders
items.description: Thirty-two rare cylinder recordings from circa 1904 in Chicago by famed collector Francis O'Neill. Historical documents of the highest importance from the dawn of sound recording in Irish traditional music. Features uilleann pipers Patsy Touhey, James Early, and Bernard Delaney, and fiddlers Edward Cronin and John McFadden.

items.title: Ceol Sidhe
items.description: An absorbing album featuring Galway musician Micheál Ó hEidhin on concertina with Charlie Lennon on fiddle and Steve Cooney on guitar. Contains 19 tracks over 70 minutes including unusual traditional dance forms - clogs, strathspeys, flings, clan marches - alongside familiar reels and jigs. A homage to local pure-drop players.

items.title: The Touch of the Master's Hand
items.description: Compilation of highlights from Mo Cheol Thú, RTÉ's long-running Sunday morning programme (1970-2005). Features poetry readings by Seamus Heaney, Ben Kiely, and MacMathúna himself, alongside musicians including Paddy Glackin, Liam O'Flynn, Denis Murphy, and Donal Lunny. Notably placed in the Irish charts between Bruce Springsteen and Björk.

items.title: Irish Traditional Guitar Accompaniment
items.description: Companion CD to the essential guide to traditional Irish music accompaniment on guitar. Gavin Ralston demonstrates exercises and tunes including alternative chords, flamenco rolls, and capo slide key changing. Features live performances with Gerry O'Connor on banjo and Paul McNevin on fiddle.

items.title: On The Leitrim Mountainside
items.description: Rosie Stewart's home-brewed album of beautiful and funny ballads, featuring songs of the travelling people preserved in their unique way. Musicians include Paddy Morgan (guitar), Thomas Polland (mandolin, mandola, banjo), Neillidh Mulligan (pipes), Brendan Carson (flute, whistle), and Petesy Burns (basses).

items.title: Traditional Fiddle Music from Donegal
items.description: Over two hours of music from Con Cassidy (1909-1994), a central figure of the Donegal fiddle tradition. Features 36 tracks including duets with Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh and Dermot McLaughlin. Con didn't come from a musical family but was surrounded by fiddlers in south-west Donegal and took to the fiddle naturally.

items.title: Love of My Life
items.description: Fiddle music from the Longford/Leitrim region by Irene Guckian, a traditional musician with a special interest in the music of her home region historically known as 'Conmhaicne'. Irene teaches fiddle at the Joe Mooney Summer School in Drumshanbo and plays with the Abhann Glas Céilí Band.

items.title: Travelling Show
items.description: Dervish's tenth album, produced by legendary John Reynolds. Came with surprises including a cover of Sonny & Cher's 'Gypsies, Tramps and Thieves' and Suzanne Vega's 'The Queen and the Soldier'. Features new arrangements of rare traditional songs such as 'Lord Levett' with harpsichord by Tríona Ní Dhomhnaill.

items.title: Humdinger
items.description: The first ever CD of Irish traditional music on melodeon and tenor banjo, evoking the atmosphere of the Irish dance hall era. Features the brilliant piano playing of Ryan Molloy and percussion of Tommy Hayes. Both multiple All-Ireland champions, Brock and Scahill breathe new life into old tunes inspired by melodeon player John Kimmell.

items.title: Within a Mile of Kilty
items.description: Traditional music from North Leitrim featuring fiddle players from the small border village of Kiltyclogher (population ~150). From this village and its environs, a unique cluster of traditional musicians have emerged over eighty years. The styles are varied yet share a common thread, with fiddle and flute at the core sound.

items.title: Two Sligo Masters
items.description: Fifth in the Coleman Centre archive series, combining reissues of two classic Shanachie LPs: 'John Vesey & Paul Brady: The First Month Of Spring' and 'Eddie Cahill: Ah! Surely'. Remastered 2-CD set showcasing the Sligo fiddle tradition from two of its finest exponents.

items.title: Many's a Wild Night
items.description: Jackie Daly's second solo album, beautifully revealing the art of accordion playing at its best. Described by the New York Times as 'probably the best accordionist in Ireland'. Features Máire O'Keeffe on fiddle, Paul de Grae on guitar, and Garry O'Briain on mandocello. Title track from Sliabh Luachra fiddler Pádraig O'Keeffe.

items.title: The Lark's Air
items.description: Dan Brouder's debut album born out of years of listening and learning from past and present masters. From Monagea in west Limerick near the Sliabh Luachra boundary, Dan plays an airy but expressive button box. Features 14 tracks including jigs, slides, reels, hornpipes, airs, flings, and polkas with strong sympathetic accompaniment.

items.title: The Friendly Visit
items.description: The second solo album from Leitrim flute player Noel Sweeney. Features a nice mixture of old and new tunes, including very local ones. Noel also plays saxophone on tracks 3 and 14. Album named after the traditional hornpipe 'The Friendly Visit'.

items.title: Blasta
items.description: Compilation of outstanding tracks from the Gael Linn Music Archive. 'Blasta' means 'Delicious' in Irish. Features 14 memorable tracks from Ireland's foremost interpreters of traditional music including Dé Dannan, Clannad, Tony MacMahon & Noel Hill, Mary Bergin, Dolores Keane, and Paddy Glackin.

items.title: Live in New York
items.description: The third in Noel Hill's instrument-defining Irish Concertina series. Ten years since Irish Concertina 2, Noel came through enormous challenges including a severe assault. Though still physically difficult, play he must. Features 13 tracks tackling big tunes like The Gold Ring, The Collier's Reel, and Banish Misfortune.

items.title: A Musical Trip to Coleman Country
items.description: Second recording by Coleman Country musicians as their contribution to building the Coleman Heritage Centre. Features some of the finest traditional music from South Sligo including the Swallows Tail Céilí Band, Seamus Tansey, John Carty, Colm O'Donnell, and PJ Hernon. Nice mix of tunes and songs with a five-flute reel.

items.title: Drumshanbows
items.description: A celebration of traditional music from Leitrim and the surrounding hinterland, released March 2014. This fiddle duet features some of Rónán's compositions with guests Paddy Ryan (fiddle), Mick Blake (piano), Ciaran Curran (bouzouki), and Tommy Murray (vocals). Launched by Ben Lennon at the Joe Mooney Summer School.

items.title: Reelin' in Tradition
items.description: Third album from the Mulcahy family of Abbeyfeale, West Limerick. Mick is a living legend in accordion and melodeon playing; Louise is in the forefront of uilleann pipers; Michelle was TG4 Young Traditional Musician of the Year 2006. Martin Hayes wrote: 'the Mulcahy Family must rank as one of the most impressive musical families in Ireland.'

items.title: Ar Nós na Gaoithe
items.description: John Wynne's second solo album, nine years after 'With Every Breath'. An absorbing collection of fourteen virtuosic tracks played with precision, discernment and breathtaking control. The Roscommon flute player sensed his playing had turned a corner. Features popular session tunes and new compositions from Tommy Peoples and Ed Reavy.

items.title: Pride of the West
items.description: Duet album drawing on the immense flute and fiddle tradition of north Connacht. Tunes mainly from the Sligo-Roscommon repertoire including unusual local versions and new compositions. Enhanced by Arty McGlynn's guitar and Paddy McEvoy's piano. Echoes of the classic Fred Finn and Peter Horan duet are discernible.

items.title: Drimalost and Beyond
items.description: 2-CD set with 73 tracks of traditional fiddle music from Danny Meehan, born in Drimalost near Mountcharles, Co. Donegal. Recorded since his return home in 2007, mostly in the old Meehan house where his musical life began. CD1 features mostly solo fiddle; CD2 includes older recordings from London. 36-page booklet included.

items.title: Brock McGuire Band
items.description: Album from the acclaimed Irish folk group fronted by button accordionist Paul Brock and fiddler Manus McGuire. Co-founded in 2000, both musicians have been at the forefront of Irish music. Named 'Traditional Irish Band of the Decade' by Irish American News in 2011. Repertoire emphasizes Irish music with American Old Timey and French-Canadian pieces.

items.title: Seán Ryan
items.description: Double CD of compositions and traditional tunes from master fiddler Seán Ryan (1919-1985) of Nenagh, Co. Tipperary. Winner of the Oireachtas 1954 and Senior All Ireland Fiddle Championship 1955 and 1956. A prolific composer of some 250 tunes. Kitchen recordings by Fr. John Quinn from the 1960s. CD1 features well-known tunes; CD2 his own compositions.

items.title: The Sound of Coleman Country
items.description: Excellent compilation of recordings from South Sligo, the area associated with legendary fiddler Michael Coleman (1891-1945). Features Carmel Gunning, Peter Horan, Colm O'Donnell, Junior Davey, Swallow Tail Ceili Band, Kevin Henry, PJ Hernon, and many other musicians from the region.

items.title: Dance of the Honey Bees
items.description: Titled 'Dúchas Ceoil: Dance of the Honey Bees', featuring 17 tracks of music and song of North Leitrim. The extended Lennon family including Charlie Lennon's sons Séan and Dónal, and nephews Maurice and Brian Lennon. Title track composed by Charlie Lennon, inspired by honeybees around Howth - the waltz symbolizes bees buzzing from flower to flower.

items.title: Tóg é go Bog é
items.description: The band's breakthrough album, their 5th time in the studio. Kíla fuse their own melodies and Gaelic lyrics with Irish instruments (whistles, uilleann pipes, bodhrán) with djembe, congas, drums, and guitars. The single 'Ón Taobh Tuathail Amach' was the first Irish language top thirty hit since Clannad's 'Harry's Game' in 1985.

items.title: Pathway to the Well
items.description: A musical journey through North Connaught with Matt Molloy on flute, John Carty on fiddle, and legendary Arty McGlynn on guitar. Recorded live at Matt Molloy's pub in Westport. 'This album is so good, words cannot fully describe it. Probably the best album of 2007' - brilliant and un-self-conscious yet incredibly thoughtful trad.

items.title: Sets in Stone
items.description: Finely calibrated collection from Dervish's Liam Kelly and Sligo fiddler Philip Duffy. Features Sligo fiddle masters' influences (Coleman, Morrison, Killoran) with lesser-known local heroes Kevin Henry, John Egan, and Jim Coleman. Joined by Shane McGowan and John Joe Kelly. Launched at the Joe Mooney Summer School, Drumshanbo.

items.title: The Gloaming 2
items.description: The second album from Irish supergroup The Gloaming, featuring Martin Hayes (fiddle), Dennis Cahill (guitar), Iarla Ó Lionáird (vocals), Caoimhín Ó Raghallaigh (hardanger d'amore), and Thomas Bartlett (piano). Debuted at #1 in the Irish Album Chart with critical acclaim.

items.title: Scaoil Amach an Pocaide - Live in Spiddal
items.description: A live album recorded in Spiddal in 2005, featuring the legendary accordion player Tony Mac Mahon with guitarist Steve Cooney. This recording serves as a fitting valediction from Mac Mahon, who retired from public performance shortly after.

items.title: Michael Coleman 1891-1945
items.description: A comprehensive double CD collection of fiddle master Michael Coleman's complete commercial recordings from 1921-1936. Includes 48 remastered tracks and an extensive biography by Harry Bradshaw with photographs and interviews.

items.title: The Music and Life of John McKenna: The Buck from the Mountain
items.description: A definitive two-disc collection of flute master John McKenna's complete commercial recordings from 1922-1937. Accompanied by a comprehensive 106-page booklet featuring biographical information, family photos, technique analysis, and music transcriptions.

items.title: Music of Sligo
items.description: A legendary flute-fiddle duet album featuring material recorded from 1959-1985 across multiple sessions. Compiled by Comhaltas Ceoltóirí Éireann and released two years after Fred Finn's death in 1986.

items.title: All the Way Home
items.description: The debut solo album from Cathy Jordan, longtime vocalist with the band Dervish. Produced in Sweden by Roger Tallroth, featuring guest appearances from Andy Irvine, Michael McGoldrick, and members of Dervish.

items.title: The Open Door
items.description: The Coleman Archive Volume 3 featuring recordings from 1929-1984. Emphasizes musicians from south Sligo and north Connacht traditions, compiled by Gregory Daly and supported by Sligo County Council.

items.title: Spirit
items.description: Dedicated to friend and sound engineer Finn Corrigan, featuring traditional Irish favorites alongside Bob Dylan's 'Boots of Spanish Leather'. The band delivers their signature blend of vocals, fiddle, flute, accordion, and bouzouki.

items.title: Siúil Uait - Take the Air
items.description: Whistle virtuoso Seán Ryan accompanied by Johnny McDonagh on bodhrán, Brendan Regan on bouzouki, Patsy Broderick on piano, Caroline Lavelle on cello, and Arty McGlynn on guitar. Features intricate traditional dance tunes and airs.

items.title: Ordinary Man
items.description: Christy Moore's tenth studio album, originally withdrawn and reissued due to controversy over 'They Never Came Home'. Features backing vocals by Enya on several tracks. A landmark album in Irish folk music.

items.title: Invocation
items.description: A revised edition of the 1994 classic featuring ethereal choral arrangements of Irish and Celtic material, along with new pieces including 'The Wild Song'. Conducted by Michael McGlynn.

items.title: The Very Best of the Dubliners
items.description: A comprehensive compilation of 27 tracks from Ireland's most beloved folk band, featuring classics like 'Whiskey in the Jar', 'Seven Drunken Nights', and 'The Irish Rover'.

items.title: After the Morning
items.description: A five-star Irish Times album featuring Roscommon piano accordionist Alan Kelly with Irish, Scottish, Breton and Asturian influences. Guest appearances by Eddi Reader and Kris Drever.

items.title: The Bunch of Keys
items.description: A two-disc educational compilation showcasing piano accordion styles and regional influences from performers worldwide. Conceived following the 2011 Munster Fleadh, compiled by Philip Clarke.

items.title: Turning the Tune
items.description: A double CD of fiddle music from Leitrim composer Charlie Lennon. The first disc features traditional tunes referencing masters like Coleman and McKenna; the second disc presents Charlie's own compositions.

items.title: Number 6
items.description: The final album from legendary Chieftains tin whistle player Sean Potts, recorded in 2010 with all proceeds donated to Na Píobairí Uilleann. Features Seán Óg Potts on pipes and Paddy Glackin on fiddle.

items.title: Fiddling without a Bow
items.description: The critically acclaimed debut solo album from Sligo button accordionist Daithí Gormley. Joe Burke described Daithí as a 'master musician.' Features Catherine McHugh on piano and Sorcha Costello on fiddle.

items.title: Banjoísta
items.description: The debut album from one of Ireland's finest banjo players, featuring 16 tracks of traditional Irish tunes. Described by the Irish Echo as 'dripping with soul' with brilliant phrasing and fabulous tone.

items.title: Bríd Harper
items.description: The long-awaited solo debut from Donegal fiddler Bríd Harper, one of the finest fiddlers of her generation. Described as 'solo fiddle of the most exquisite kind' and one of the best traditional albums of 2015.

items.title: Stone Walls & Street Lights
items.description: An album by the Irish traditional music group New Road.

items.title: The Dusty Bridge
items.description: Irish fiddler Fergal Scahill's 2008 album recorded with pianist Ryan Molloy. Features traditional and contemporary compositions from Máirtín O'Connor, Charlie Lennon, Josephine Keegan, and Paddy Fahey.

items.title: Around and About
items.description: A 2012 recording by accomplished Irish traditional musician and prolific composer Josephine Keegan, featuring her own compositions on fiddle and piano.

items.title: Consider the Source
items.description: The Irish Echo's Album of the Year (2008), featuring New York-born Sligo-style fiddler Brian Conway with guest appearances by Niamh Parsons, Dan Milner, Billy McComiskey, and Joannie Madden. A tribute to his musical mentors.

items.title: In the Footsteps of Ó Coleman
items.description: A compilation celebrating the legacy of master fiddler Michael Coleman and the rich Sligo music tradition he represented.

items.title: Roots of the Banjo Tree
items.description: The Irish Times Traditional Music Album of the Year (2012). Galway family band's debut blending Irish traditional music with bluegrass and old-time Appalachian tunes in a distinctive 'Celt-grass' style.

items.title: The Well Tempered Bow
items.description: A fiddle duet album from the Kane Sisters of Letterfrack, Connemara. Their style is heavily influenced by South Sligo and East Galway traditions, particularly the music of composer Paddy Fahey. Described as 'fine-honed unison fiddling.'

items.title: All Things Considered
items.description: Winner of Best Live Act at the BBC Folk Awards and Best Overall Traditional Irish Band from the Irish Music Awards. A powerful collection showcasing Danu's distinctive blend of instrumental prowess and vocal excellence.

items.title: The Rocks of Bawn
items.description: The third recording under the Coleman Heritage label featuring well-known singer, lilter and traditional musician Colm O'Donnell, holder of 30 All-Ireland medals. Twelve songs in sean-nós style plus two lilted tracks.

items.title: Born for Sport
items.description: A gorgeous pairing of fiddle and flute from two masters of Irish traditional music. Former Altan member Paul O'Shaughnessy (Donegal style fiddle) joins flute virtuoso Harry Bradley. Irish Times four-star review praised the 'immediacy and freshness of the playing.'

items.title: The Fire Aflame
items.description: A supergroup collaboration featuring three legendary Chieftains members: Matt Molloy on flute, Seán Keane on fiddle, and Liam O'Flynn on uilleann pipes. Guest musicians include Arty McGlynn, Neil Martin, and Noreen O'Donoghue.

items.title: New Road
items.description: The second solo recording from North Kerry piper Leonard Barry, featuring traditional music from North Kerry, Sliabh Luachra, and West Limerick. Irish Times praised his 'disciplined style' and 'cut-glass detail.' Features John Carty, Rick Epping, Seamie O'Dowd, and Cathy Jordan.

items.title: The Home Place
items.description: The Coleman Archive Volume 2 featuring archival recordings from old 78' records. Showcases both well-known and lesser-known musicians from the Sligo tradition, preserving the rich north Connacht musical heritage.

items.title: The Given Note
items.description: The fourth solo album from master uilleann piper Liam O'Flynn, produced by Shaun Davey. Title suggested by Seamus Heaney. Dedicated to Leo Rowsome on the 25th anniversary of his death. AllMusic calls it 'an object lesson in the way Celtic music should be played.'

items.title: Bringing It All Back Home
items.description: A double CD remastered from the acclaimed 1991 BBC/RTÉ documentary series produced by Donal Lunny. Explores the profound influence of Irish music on American and world music traditions as emigrants took their music abroad.

items.title: The Cook in the Kitchen
items.description: Accordion music from Kerry featuring 14 selections of Irish traditional music from the famous North Kerry musical tradition. John Brosnan, a well-reputed repairman and reed tuner, demonstrates his playing skills accompanied by Celia Regan on piano.

items.title: The McDonaghs of Ballinafad and Friends
items.description: House recordings from the 1960s featuring the legendary McDonagh brothers from near Ballinafad, Sligo. Their unique style preceded the Coleman/Morrison era, retaining elements of an older tradition. Features 37 tracks with guest appearances by Tommy Flynn and others.

items.title: The Genius of Peter Conlon
items.description: 78 RPM recordings from 1917-1929 by the foremost player of traditional Irish music on the melodeon during the golden age of recording. P.J. Conlon was one of the earliest Irish-born musicians to record. Includes 44-page booklet documenting his life and legacy.

items.title: Skins & Sins
items.description: A rare album featuring bodhrán as the main instrument. Inaugural winner of The World Bodhrán Championships (2006) Neill Lyons is joined by fifteen talented musicians. Praised for raising the standing of the often-abused instrument with 'not one false beat or weak track.'

items.title: Friends of Note
items.description: Traditional Irish music on flute and whistles from the Donagher family of Easkey, County Sligo. Aileen Donagher is a multi-instrumentalist prominent in musical circles throughout the West of Ireland.

items.title: Amhráin as Árainn agus Conamara
items.description: Songs from the Aran Islands and Connemara featuring two Corn Uí Riada winners. Treasa Ní Mhiolláin (1979 winner) from Aran and Mícheál Seoighe (1972 winner) from Conamara perform 12 traditional songs originally recorded in 1973. CD reissue from 2015.
